summary: Slope measures steepness (rise over run), and it's the key to writing and interpreting the equation of any line.
explanation: Slope is "rise over run": the change in \(y\) divided by the change in \(x\) between any two points on the line. In slope-intercept form \(y=mx+b\), \(m\) is the slope and \(b\) is the \(y\)-intercept (where the line crosses the \(y\)-axis, i.e. where \(x=0\)). Parallel lines have equal slopes; perpendicular lines have slopes that are negative reciprocals of each other (their product is \(-1\)). A horizontal line has slope 0; a vertical line has undefined slope (division by zero in the rise/run formula). To find where two lines intersect, set their equations equal and solve, or use substitution/elimination as with any system of equations.
